description: Codes and standards governing steel erection — AISC, AWS D1.1, RCSC bolt specification, OSHA Subpart R, SSPC coating standards, and ASME rigging standards.

Code_of_Standard_Practice.description: AISC 303 — THE reference for fabrication and erection tolerances, responsibilities, and practices.

key_sections.section_6: Fabrication — mill tolerances, shop tolerances, cambering, bolt holes.
key_sections.section_7: Erection — erection tolerances, plumbing, temporary bracing, field connections.
key_sections.table_7_1: Erection tolerances — column plumbness, beam elevation, sweep. The specific numbers inspectors measure against.

Code_of_Standard_Practice.practical_note: When an inspector measures your column plumbness, they are checking against AISC 303 Table 7.1. Know these numbers.

AISC_360.description: Specification for Structural Steel Buildings. Design standard — engineers use this.
AISC_360.relevance_to_field: Chapter J covers connection design that the field must execute. Chapter N covers quality control and quality assurance.

inspection_categories.Category_A: Visual inspection by contractor QC. Most connections.
inspection_categories.Category_B: Visual inspection plus UT/MT/PT by independent QA. CJP welds on moment connections.

AISC_341.description: Seismic Provisions for Structural Steel Buildings. Applies in seismic design categories C, D, E, F.
AISC_341.field_impact: Demand-critical welds, protected zones (no holes, no attachments), special connection requirements. More stringent welding and inspection.

AISC_358.description: Prequalified Connections for Special and Intermediate Steel Moment Frames.
AISC_358.field_impact: Defines approved moment connection designs — reduced beam section (dogbone), bolted unstiffened/stiffened extended end plate, etc. Welder must follow these details exactly.

AWS_D1_1.description: Structural Welding Code — Steel. Governs ALL structural welding on steel buildings and structures.

key_provisions.WPS_qualification: Section 4. All welding must be per a qualified WPS. Prequalified or qualification-tested.
key_provisions.welder_qualification: Section 4. All welders must be tested and qualified for the positions and processes they will use. Qualification valid per Section 4 rules (continuity typically requires welding within 6 months).
key_provisions.inspection: Section 6. Visual inspection criteria, UT acceptance criteria, RT acceptance criteria. This is what the inspector reads when deciding accept or reject.
key_provisions.prequalified_joints: Section 3. Joint details that are accepted without testing. Bevel angles, root openings, root faces, effective throats. Most field joints use prequalified details.

visual_acceptance.fusion: Complete fusion required between weld and base metal and between weld passes.
visual_acceptance.undercut: Primary members: 1/32 inch max. Secondary: 1/16 inch max.
visual_acceptance.porosity: CJP groove: shall not exceed criteria in Table 6.1. Fillet: sum of diameters shall not exceed 3/8 inch in any linear inch.
visual_acceptance.profiles: Fillet: convex or flat. Groove: flush to 1/8 inch reinforcement max (butt joints).

AWS_D1_1.practical_note: Every structural welder should have access to AWS D1.1 on the job. When an inspector rejects a weld, they will cite the specific D1.1 clause. Knowing the code helps you dispute questionable rejections and avoid legitimate ones.

RCSC.description: Research Council on Structural Connections — Specification for Structural Joints Using High-Strength Bolts.

key_provisions.snug_tight: Section 8.1. Plies in firm contact. A few impacts of impact wrench or full effort of one person.
key_provisions.pretensioned: Section 8.2. Minimum tension per Table 8.1. Methods: turn-of-nut, calibrated wrench, TC bolt, DTI.
key_provisions.slip_critical: Section 8.3. Same tensioning as pretensioned PLUS faying surface requirements (Class A or B slip coefficient).
key_provisions.installation: Bolts must be installed from snug-tight condition. All bolts in a joint snugged before pretensioning begins. Pretensioning progresses systematically from most rigid part of joint outward.
key_provisions.reuse: A325: may be reused if approved by Engineer of Record. A490: SHALL NOT be reused. TC bolts: single use only (spline is gone).

washers.hardened: F436 hardened washer required under the element being turned (nut or bolt head) for pretensioned and slip-critical.
washers.beveled: Required when outer face of bolted parts has slope greater than 1:20 from perpendicular to bolt axis.
washers.DTI: F959 Direct Tension Indicator washer is placed under the stationary element (not the turned element).

OSHA_1926_Subpart_R.description: Safety Standards for Steel Erection. OSHA 1926.750 through 1926.761.

key_rules.1926_752: Site layout and construction sequence. Erection plan, adequate access roads, firm footing for cranes.
key_rules.1926_753: Hoisting and rigging. Load charts, sling inspection, tag lines.
key_rules.1926_754: Structural steel assembly. Anchor bolts (min 4 per column), column stability, beam connection (min 2 bolts), double connections.
key_rules.1926_755: Column anchorage. Minimum 4 anchor bolts. Repair anchor bolts only with engineer approval.
key_rules.1926_756: Beams and columns. Plumbing-up by temporary bracing. Double connection procedures.
key_rules.1926_757: Open web steel joists. Erection bridging before releasing crane. Attachment before any load.
key_rules.1926_760: Fall protection. 100% protection above 15 feet for connectors (if qualified), 6 feet for all others. Controlled access zone option.
key_rules.1926_761: Training. All workers in steel erection must be trained in fall protection, connection procedures, hazard recognition.

OSHA_1926_Subpart_R.practical_note: OSHA Subpart R is law, not a guideline. Violations result in fines (,000+ per serious violation) and potential criminal charges if a death occurs. Every ironworker foreman must know these rules.

SSPC.description: Society for Protective Coatings (formerly Steel Structures Painting Council). Standards for surface preparation and coating.

surface_prep.SP1: Solvent cleaning. Remove oil, grease, dirt. Required before any blast cleaning.
surface_prep.SP2: Hand tool cleaning. Wire brush, scraping, sanding. Minimum preparation — use only where better prep is not possible.
surface_prep.SP3: Power tool cleaning. Grinders, wire wheels, needle guns. Better than SP2, not as good as blast.
surface_prep.SP6: Commercial blast cleaning. 66% clean. Acceptable for many shop primers.
surface_prep.SP7: Brush-off blast. Lightest blast — removes loose material only.
surface_prep.SP10: Near-white blast. 95% clean. Standard for most industrial coating systems.
surface_prep.SP11: Power tool cleaning to bare metal. Equivalent to near-white but by mechanical means. Used for field touch-up where blasting is not practical.
surface_prep.SP5: White metal blast. 100% clean. Highest level. For immersion service or where spec demands it.

SSPC.SSPC_PA2: Measurement of Dry Film Thickness with Magnetic Gauges. The procedure for DFT measurement — how many readings, where to take them, how to calculate compliance.
SSPC.practical_note: Surface preparation is 80% of coating success. The best paint in the world will fail on poorly prepared steel. Spend the time on prep.

ASME_B30.description: Standards for cranes, hoists, rigging, and material handling.

key_volumes.B30_5: Mobile and Locomotive Cranes. Load charts, inspection, operation.
key_volumes.B30_9: Slings. Rated capacities, inspection criteria, usage.
key_volumes.B30_10: Hooks. Inspection, throat opening, twist, load rating.
key_volumes.B30_26: Rigging Hardware. Shackles, links, rings, turnbuckles.

ASME_B30.signal_person: ASME B30.5 defines standard hand signals. These are the signals used industry-wide. Qualification required per OSHA.
ASME_B30.annual_inspection: All cranes, slings, and rigging hardware must be inspected annually (or more frequently for heavy use). Documented inspection records required.
